reasoning: I have spent five years watching both the BJP and Mamata's government treat democratic institutions as personal property—the Centre weaponizing its agencies, Mamata treating the state as her fiefdom. The Left-Congress alliance remains the only available vehicle for opposition to Modi's communal authoritarianism without capitulating to Mamata's dynastic disorder. I hold no illusions: the Left is diminished and imperfect, but voting for it is not enthusiasm for them; it is a structural choice to keep both evils in check. I cannot vote BJP—Shantanu's CAA promise was constitutional violation dressed as deliverance—and I will not legitimize Mamata's opportunism by voting Biswajit Das, who has worn three jerseys and represents everything rotten about how she treats the state. I vote Left with my nose held, as I said I would.
primary_drivers: Opposition to BJP's communal authoritarianism and state weaponization
Refusal to legitimize Mamata's treatment of the state as personal property
Structural commitment to Left-Congress as the only available principled alternative
